title: [phase:plan] [family:orchestrator] startup bootstrap plan lane for the current runtime startup canary. Do not inspect the repository. Stay scoped to canary lane activation only.
summary_text: # Plan Bootstrap a startup canary lane for the current runtime only, with the lane disabled by default and activated through an explicit gate. The approach is to define a minimal, isolated startup path that proves lane activation, emits a clear health signal, and fails safely without changing broader orchestration behavior. ## Scope - In: startup canary lane definition, activation gate, minimal startup health checks, safe failure behavior, validation of active vs inactive paths - Out: repository-wide runtime refactors, non-canary lane changes, provider-policy changes beyond canary activation, unrelated workflow or control-plane behavior ## Action items [ ] Define the startup canary lane contract: activation signal, entrypoint, expected startup checks, success criteria, and fail/abort conditions. [ ] Add an explicit canary activation gate that keeps the lane off by default and makes activation auditable and reversible. [ ] Isolate lane selection so startup can run the canary lane without mutating the normal lane matrix when the gate is not set. [ ] Add minimal canary startup checks that prove the runtime bootstraps correctly, resolves the intended startup path, and emits a single clear success/failure signal. [ ] Add fail-fast timeout and non-cascading behavior so a canary startup failure does not silently promote into broader execution. [ ] Emit structured startup telemetry for the canary lane only, including activation reason, start time, outcome, and rollback/off-switch state. [ ] Verify two paths: default startup with canary disabled remains unchanged, and gated startup with canary enabled activates only the canary lane and exits with the expected status. [ ] Define rollout and rollback procedure for the canary lane, including who can enable it, how long it should remain active, and the immediate disable path if startup health degrades. ## Open questions - What should be the single source of truth for canary activation: environment variable, issue/dispatch input, or dedicated runtime flag? - Should a startup canary failure block startup entirely, or only mark the canary lane unhealthy while preserving the normal startup path? - What is the required success signal for this canary: log/artifact only, status check, or issue/comment audit trail?
